返回本批次

“寻求联系”：视频游戏中玩家停止射击，开始说话

'Seeking connection': video game where players stopped shooting, started talking

www.theguardian.com 作者 andsoitis 分数 57 评论 65 2026/04/15 17:31
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 电子游戏《Arc Raiders》以人类末日般的未来的致命想象为背景。幸存者被迫住在殖民地的地下深处，而神秘、凶残的人工智能机器则在地表巡逻。只有昔日城市的荒凉废墟得以幸存，而鲁莽的人类“袭击者”则前往地面执行危险的搜寻任务。尽管这些被称为 Arcs 的武装机器人带来了诸多威胁，但在这款广受欢迎的游戏中，致命的机器人并不是最大的威胁，该游戏于去年年底发布，销量已超过 1400 万份。袭击者总是担心其他人会一看到他们就开枪射击并偷走他们的战利品。在这种竞争激烈、高风险的世界里，无情是有回报的。因此，瑞典 Embark Studios 的游戏开发者感到震惊的是，许多玩家根本没有互相射击。 “这让我们有点惊讶，”执行制作人 Aleksander Grøndal 说道，他发现很多人玩的游戏“比我们预期的更加和平”。他很快补充道：“澄清一下，令人惊喜。”不知不觉中，这款游戏已经成为一种社会和心理实验，提出了有关游戏设计和人类状况的问题，引起了社会科学家、心理学家和犯罪学家的兴趣。大约五分之一的玩家从未击倒过其他袭击者，一半的玩家击倒的袭击者少于 10 名。 名为 Arcs 的神秘机器人在地表巡逻。照片：Embark Studios 在大多数射击游戏中，从《堡垒之夜》到《反恐精英》，杀死其他玩家都是重点，也是赚取积分的方式。 （Embark 的许多开发团队都拥有其他快节奏射击游戏的经验，包括大型《战地》和《使命召唤》系列游戏。）而《Arc Raiders》属于不断发展的射击游戏子类型的一部分，这些游戏以残酷而闻名：撤离射击游戏，在这款游戏中，玩家不仅要相互竞争，还要与世界本身竞争，争分夺秒地活着离开每一轮，并完好无损地逃脱每一轮。游戏过程非常激烈，风险与回报较高，在努力收集战利品后，死亡往往会降临，因为你会被另一名试图窃取战利品的玩家伏击。那么，为什么《Arc Raiders》的玩家没有像环境所要求的那样无情呢？Grøndal 表示，团队知道存在一些合作的空间。 “我们一直希望情况如此，但看到有多少人关注游戏的这一方面，有点令人惊讶……这有点让整个撤离射击游戏大开眼界，因为它并不总是与其他玩家发生冲突。”合作的催化剂……Arc Raiders 的女族长。照片：Embark Studios 在这个饱受蹂躏的世界里，人们除了互相射击之外还在做什么？许多人正在联手消灭机器人怪物，这些机器人怪物的范围从飞行的无人机到会爆炸的球体。其他人则试图悄悄潜入他们周围以掠夺稀有资源。格伦达尔说，球员们还会自发举办狂欢派对，人们通过麦克风播放音乐。但通常情况下，球员们只是在说话。 YouTube 上的一段视频名为《The Humans of Arc Raiders》，灵感来自一位在纽约采访陌生人的摄影师，其中包括与随机遇到的玩家的对话。他们谈论家庭斗争、工作生活、抑郁症、自闭症，还有一次肺衰竭。在一次对话中，一位身穿绿色盔甲、全副武装、名叫 Poopy 的玩家坦率地问另一位袭击者：“老兄，养孩子是什么感觉？”当我第一次进入《Arc Raiders》时，我发现了上层的二分法，鸟儿在歌唱，植物在被击落的机器残骸中茁壮成长。我在这个 1970 年代风格的复古未来环境中徘徊得越多，遇到的其他人就越多，其中许多人提供了帮助，例如医疗用品。大多数时候，我们一起偷偷摸摸地与机器人作战。有时很紧张，有时很可怕，但常常很放松。 在一次比赛中，我遇到了另一位操着英国口音的玩家，他也是这个游戏的新手。 “你被别人杀了吗？”当我们探索一个爆裂的混凝土大坝建筑群时，他问我。 “因为我遇到的每个人都很友好，”他补充道。 “没有人互相残杀。”玩家停止互相射击的电子游戏我一定已经习惯了人与人之间缺乏暴力，因为第一次被另一个人淘汰时，我感到很生气。显然，我被人类友情的温暖感觉所吸引，很难不感到不安。就好像我的攻击者打破了一条不成文的规则，即我们都应该互相帮助。事实上，这就是 Embark 最初希望创造的游戏的目标——共同对抗机器，而人类玩家在身体上无法互相对抗。但在开发过程的后期，他们认为这会变得无聊，因此对其进行了更改，添加了不可预测的人类和有趣的是，《Arc Raiders》中有很多口头交流，玩家使用麦克风的次数比其他游戏要多得多。在 Arc Raiders 中，玩家可以听到附近的任何其他人的声音，让他们大喊“我很友好！”或“平安！平安！”。 Grøndal 表示，超过 95% 的玩家使用这种近距离聊天功能。许多玩家仍然会看到视线就射击，但他们只是少数。 Embark 告诉《卫报》，大约 30% 的玩家最感兴趣的是游戏的合作方面；另外 30% 关注玩家对玩家的动作；剩下的 40% 则两者兼而有之。单独玩的人通常更友好，而三人小队的人对交火更感兴趣。 来自田纳西州的图形艺术家 Sean Hensley 制作了有关心理健康和电子游戏的 YouTube 视频，他对这款游戏很感兴趣，并认为玩家更看重“联系而不是竞争”。他在最近的视频中表示：“玩家从这些友好的互动中获得的回报比任何游戏战利品系统或胜利屏幕都更有价值。”然而，最有力的合作催化剂可能是共同的威胁。当《出发》中引入了一种名为“女族长”的大型步行机械敌人时，格兰达尔预计对手小队会很狡猾，等待其他人耗尽弹药后再攻击他们以窃取战利品。相反，玩家使用近距离聊天来组队。 “瞬间——就像字面意义上的不到 30 秒——服务器上的每个人都停止了互相射击，并共同面对更大的挑战，”他说。 “我并没有真正预料到每个人都会在 30 秒的时间范围内如此轻松地合作。” 这种意想不到的玩家行为可能会给开发者带来问题，因为他们会根据他们对人们玩游戏的方式的预期来调整敌人的难度。轻易击落一个巨大的机器人就不那么有趣了，当每个人都合作时，只需要几分钟的时间。 “如果人们很容易就不再互相攻击，”Grøndal 说，“我们就需要迎接挑战。”“我们无意中为人们创造了一个交流的场所”……Arc Raiders。格伦达尔说，玩家是否想要人际关系，或者是否正在做出一个冷酷的、经过深思熟虑的决定，认为合作对所有人来说更有利可图，这是科学家的问题，而不是视频游戏开发者的问题。最近，一位犯罪学家联系了他，他说这位犯罪学家“对玩家之间的互动方式非常感兴趣”。Embark 首席执行官帕特里克·索德伦德 (Patrick Söderlund) 此前曾表示，一位神经学教授朋友拍了拍他的肩膀，这位朋友对从《Arc Raiders》中学到的有关人类行为的课程感兴趣。但他有自己的理论，植根于现代流行的孤立和孤独。 “我认为人们正在寻求与其他玩家的联系，也许这在现实世界中不再那么容易做到，因为人们被困在手机上，”他说。 “也许我们无意中创造了一个供人们联系的地方。”因为虚拟互动是暂时的，Grøndal 觉得游戏的功能是“一个与其他人联系的地方，也许可以在没有恐惧、影响或判断的情况下敞开心扉”。这与我自己在《Arc Raiders》中的经历相符，我在那里遇到了很多人，但通常只有几分钟，然后他们或我就消失在荒野中。《Arc Raiders》当然不是它最初看起来的那样。虽然未来看起来一片黯淡，人类仍在苦苦挣扎，但这里却充满希望。 “是的，弧线已经占领了地表，并且占据了主导地位。但如果你环顾四周，你会发现大自然已经从生态崩溃中恢复过来，”格伦达尔说。 “动物回来了，世界欣欣向荣。我们希望给玩家灌输希望。”

Original Summary

  1. The video game Arc Raiders is set in a lethal imagining of an apocalyptic future for humanity. Survivors have been forced to live deep underground in colonies while mysterious, murderous AI machines patrol the surface. Only the desolate ruins of former cities survive, and reckless human “raiders” take trips topside to conduct dangerous scavenging missions.For all the menace of these armed robots, called Arcs, the deadly droids are not the biggest threat in this hugely popular game, which was released late last year and has sold more than 14m copies. Raiders operate with the constant anxiety that another person will shoot them on sight and steal their loot. Mercilessness is rewarded in this kind of competitive, high-stakes world.So it has come as a jolt to the game’s developers at Embark Studios in Sweden that many players are not shooting at each other at all. “It caught us a little bit by surprise,” says executive producer Aleksander Grøndal, who has found that many people play “a more peaceful version of the game than we anticipated”. He is quick to add: “Pleasantly surprised, just to be clear.”Unintentionally, the game has become a sort of social and psychological experiment, raising questions about game design – and the human condition – that have intrigued social scientists, psychologists and criminologists. Roughly one in five players have never knocked out another raider, and half have knocked out fewer than 10.Mysterious robots, called Arcs, patrol the surface. Photograph: Embark StudiosIn most shooters, from Fortnite to Counter-Strike, killing other players is the point – and the way to earn points. (Many of the development team at Embark are experienced with other fast-paced shooter games, including the massive Battlefield and Call of Duty franchises.) And Arc Raiders is part of a growing subgenre of shooting games that are notoriously cutthroat: the extraction shooter, where players compete not just with each other but with the world itself, working against the clock to get out of each round alive and with their scavenged treasure intact. Sessions are intense, with high risk v reward gameplay in which death often comes right at the end of a hard effort gathering loot, as you are ambushed by another player seeking to steal swag. So why aren’t Arc Raiders’ players behaving as mercilessly as the environment calls for?Grøndal says the team knew there was room for some cooperation. “We always wanted [that] to be the case, but it was a little bit surprising to see how many people latched on to that aspect of the game … It kind of blew the whole extraction shooter open, because it doesn’t always have to be about conflict with other players.”A catalyst for cooperation … Arc Raiders’ Matriarch. Photograph: Embark StudiosWhat are people doing instead of shooting each other in this ravaged world? Many are teaming up to take down the robot monsters, which range from flying drones to spherical balls that blast fire. Others try to sneak quietly around them to scavenge rare resources. Grøndal says players also hold spontaneous rave parties, where people play music through their microphones.But often, players are just talking. A YouTube video called The Humans of Arc Raiders, inspired by the photographer who interviews strangers in New York City, includes conversations with randomly encountered players. They talk about family struggles, work lives, depression, autism and, in one case, a lung collapse. In one conversation, a heavily armed player in green armour named Poopy candidly asks another raider: “What’s it like having kids, dude?”When I first jumped into Arc Raiders, I found a dichotomy on the topside, where birds sing and plants thrive among the carcasses of downed machines. The more I wandered around this 1970s-style retro-future setting, the more I bumped into other humans, many of whom offered help, such as medical supplies. Mostly we snuck around and battled robots together. It was tense at times, sometimes scary, but often relaxing.In one session, I encountered another player with a British accent who was also new to the game. “Have you been killed by another person yet?” he asked me, as we explored a burst concrete dam complex. “Because every person I’ve met has been friendly,” he added. “No one kills each other.”The video game where players stopped shooting each otherI must have gotten used to the lack of human-on-human violence because the first time I was eliminated by another person, I felt quite offended. Clearly, I had been lured into a warm feeling of human camaraderie, and it was hard not to be upset. It was as if my attacker had broken an unwritten rule that we were all supposed to help each other out.That was, in fact, the aim of the original game Embark had hoped to create – a shared fight against the machines where human players physically cannot fight each other. But late in the development process, they thought it would get boring, so changed it to add in unpredictable humans and the added tension that brings.Interestingly, there is a lot of spoken communication in Arc Raiders, with players using their microphones much more than in other games. In Arc Raiders, a player can hear any other people in their proximity, allowing them to shout out “I’m friendly!” or “Peaceful! Peaceful!”. More than 95% of players use this proximity chat feature, says Grøndal.Many players do still shoot on sight, but they form a minority. Embark told the Guardian that about 30% of players are mostly interested in the cooperative aspects of the game; another 30% focus on the player-versus-player action; and the remaining 40% enjoy a mix of both. Those playing solo are generally more friendly, while those who group up into squads of three are more interested in firefights.Sean Hensley, a graphic artist from Tennessee who makes YouTube videos about mental health and video games, has taken an interest in the game and believes players value “connection over competition”. “What players are getting from these friendly interactions is more rewarding than any game loot system or victory screen,” he said in a recent video.The strongest catalyst for cooperation, however, might be a common threat. When Embark introduced a massive walking mechanical enemy called the Matriarch, Grøndal expected rival squads to be sneaky, waiting for others to expend their ammunition before attacking them to steal the loot. Instead, players used proximity chat to team up. “In an instant – like literally in less than 30 seconds – everyone on that server stopped shooting each other and faced the bigger challenge together,” he said. “And I hadn’t really anticipated the fact that every single one would cooperate that easily in a 30-second timeframe.”This type of unexpected player behaviour can be problematic for developers, as they tweak an enemy’s difficulty depending on how they anticipate people will play. Easily downing a massive robot is less fun, and when everyone cooperates, it only takes a few minutes. “If it’s so easy for people to stop turning on each other,” says Grøndal, “we need to up the challenge.”‘We have accidentally created a place for people to connect’ … Arc Raiders. Photograph: Embark StudiosWhether players want human connection – or are making a cold, calculated decision that it is more profitable for all to cooperate – is a question for scientists, not video game developers, says Grøndal. He was recently contacted by a criminologist who he says was “really intrigued by how players are interacting with each other”.Embark CEO Patrick Söderlund has previously said he was tapped on the shoulder by a neurology professor friend interested in the lessons they could learn from Arc Raiders about human behaviour. But has his own theories, rooted in the modern epidemic of isolation and loneliness. “I think that people are seeking connections with other players and maybe this is not so easy to do out in the real world any more because people are stuck on their phone,” he says. “Maybe we have kind of accidentally created a place for people to connect.” Because the virtual interactions are temporary, Grøndal feels the game functions “as a place to connect with other people and maybe open up without fear or repercussions or judgment”.That tallies with my own experience in Arc Raiders, where I met plenty of people, but usually only for a few minutes, before they or I disappeared into the wilderness.Arc Raiders is certainly not what it first seems to be. While it looks like a bleak future where humanity is struggling, there is hope here. “Yes, the Arcs have captured the surface and they’re the dominant. But if you look around you, nature has come back from an ecological collapse,” Grøndal says. “The animals are back and the world is thriving. We want to instil hope in the player.”

评论（默认中文）

wafflemaker 2026/04/15 19:13
很多年前，当我在线玩《EvE》时，我对一名敌方民兵玩家感到惊讶。

背景：我们部分生活在允许 PvP 的低安全系统中。当没有 5 千人争夺系统控制权时，来自大联盟的 PvP 玩家就来到了这里。

我注意到一名敌方玩家在战斗中一直表现得非常侠义。他将战利品返还给与他打赢过的经验不足的人，并且无论失败还是胜利，他总是保持谦虚。他甚至有一次归还了一个非常昂贵的模块，这个模块是在他赢得与我的战斗后从我的船上掉落的。这清楚地表明我不是一个需要照顾的新球员。

这教会了我很多关于玩耍和生活中一般行为的知识。这并不总是与你的结果有关，而总是与你的风格有关。

When playing EvE online many years ago, I was surprised by one enemy militia player.

Context: we were living partially in low sec systems where PvP was allowed. That's where PvP players from big alliances came when there were no 5k ppl fights for system control.

I've noticed one enemy player consitently being very chivalry about fights. He returned loot to less experienced people he won a fight with and was always humble in both defeat and victory. He even once returned a very expensive module that dropped from my ship after he won a fight with me. And that showed clearly I wasn't a new player that needed taking care off.

That taught me a lot about playing and general behavior in life. That it's not always about your results, but it's always about your style.

在 HN 中查看
Groxx 2026/04/15 19:30
我的第一次“玩家遭遇”是在一个多小时内（大约 15 分钟）耐心地向我介绍了许多基础知识的人，然后诱骗我攻击他们，这样他们就可以炸死我并逃脱惩罚。他们甚至正在解释为什么有些玩家可能会这样做，但只是遗漏了重要的部分。

这就是我喜欢的巨魔。从技术上讲，他们在这个过程中教会了我另一件有用的事情，而且它实际上没有花费我任何钱，因为我可以重新滚动角色。高努力低回报是最好的。

My first "player encounter" was with someone who patiently took me through many of the basics for over an hour at the very beginning (like 15 minutes in), then tricked me into attacking them so they could blow me up and get away with it. They were even in the process of explaining why some players might do it, but just left out the important parts.

That's the kind of troll I love. Technically they taught me another useful thing in the process, and it cost me effectively nothing because I could just reroll the character. High effort and low payoff is the best kind.

在 HN 中查看
wafflemaker 2026/04/15 19:58
这可能会让你感到惊讶，但现在的教程实际上会让你大吃一惊。很奇怪的是，游戏实际上会告诉你现在该怎么做，而你不必寻求帮助。
It might surprise you, but the tutorial nowadays actually gets you blown up twice. It's so strange, that the game actually tells you what to do now and you don't have to ask in help.
在 HN 中查看
sxp 2026/04/15 22:18
低秒的运作规则与高秒的gankers或零秒的政治不同。特别是当你处于派系战争区域时。 《夏娃》中的标准观点是，如果你处于公平的战斗中，那么你就玩错了游戏。但在《FW》中，大部分战斗都是光荣的决斗。由于 FW 玩家希望在该区域进行更多战斗和更多玩家，因此为新手退款并设置平衡的决斗和重赛是很常见的。
Low sec runs by different rules from the gankers in high sec or the politics of null sec. Especially if you're in the Faction Warfare zones. The standard view in Eve is that if you're in a fair fight, you're playing the game incorrectly. But with FW, a good chunk of the fights are honorable duels. And since FW players want more fights and more players in that zone, refunding ships for newbies and setting up balanced duels and rematches are common.
在 HN 中查看
nirui 2026/04/15 21:52
> 无论失败还是胜利都始终保持谦虚

我从来没有玩过《EVE Online》，但我听说有一次，有些玩家在吉塔星系统中进行营杀新手（称为自杀式ganking），这是一个有很多活动的贸易中心。情况太糟糕了，CCP 必须禁止这样做的玩家。

另一款游戏《Elite Dangerous》也面临着ganking的问题，有些人就是无耻地这么做（从他们上传的视频来看），有些人甚至还启用了作弊功能。

基于此，我认为你所经历的事情是相当罕见的。也许这个人只是一个好人，即使在竞争激烈的环境中也愿意做好事，而你很幸运遇到这样的人。

> was always humble in both defeat and victory

I never played EVE Online much, but I've heard there was a time some player were camp-killing newbies (known as Suicide ganking) in Jita star system which is a tradehub with a lot of activities. It got so bad CCP has to ban players who's doing that.

Another game, Elite Dangerous, also faces ganking problems, some people just doing it shamelessly (judging by the videos they uploaded), some even do it with cheat enabled.

Based on that, I think what you've experienced is rather rare. Maybe the individual are just a good person who's willing to do good even in a competitive environment, and you're lucky to have encountered such person.

在 HN 中查看
adzm 2026/04/15 19:02
我很想提一下《无尽的森林》，这是一款近二十年前的艺术游戏，你是一只鹿，没有真正的目标，交流完全通过鹿的手势和声音。这个想法很棒，随机找到其他人（如鹿）并一起探索幻想世界很有趣。
I'd love to mention the Endless Forest, an artistic game from nearly twenty years ago where you were a deer and there were no real goals and communication was entirely with deer gestures and sounds. The idea was great and it was fun finding random other people (as deer) and exploring the fantasy world together.
在 HN 中查看
tetris11 2026/04/15 21:12
与《Journey》一样，你与其他无名者一起走过的美丽风景，镜像彼此的行为，并陷入一种有凝聚力的、不言而喻的群体心态
Same with Journey, a beautiful landscape that you traversed with other nameless ones, mirroring each other's actions and falling into a cohesive unspoken group mindset
在 HN 中查看
hackable_sand 2026/04/15 22:37
光之子也很甜蜜低调。同一个工作室。你的进步与社交表达息息相关，我发现这是远离竞技游戏的令人振奋的休息。
Children of the Light is also very sweet and low-key. Same studio. Your progression is tied to social expression and I find it very a uplifting break from competitive games.
在 HN 中查看
viktorcode 2026/04/15 19:06
游戏根据玩家对他人的攻击性，巧妙地将他们分类到大厅。这就是它与现实生活场景的不同之处。
The game smartly sorts players into lobbies based on their aggressiveness towards others. And this is where it diverges from real life scenarios.
在 HN 中查看
ekidd 2026/04/15 20:53
是的，ARC Raiders 有一个大厅系统，可以根据攻击性对人们进行分类。

这很有趣，因为早期的 Steam 评论喜欢这个游戏 大多 合作的。大多数玩家都是乐于助人或中立的，但有些玩家会试图杀死你。这意味着遇到其他玩家是 紧张。他们会医治你吗？他们会帮你拆掉机器吗？或者他们会抢劫你？你必须猜，而且猜得很快。

然后，大批 Twitch 观众涌入观看 PvP。

事实上，我认为“大部分是合作但并不总是”的动态是一种非常有趣的氛围，但对于开发人员来说可能很难维持。

Yeah, ARC Raiders has a lobby system to sort people by aggressiveness.

Which is interesting, because the early Steam reviews loved the fact that the game was mostly cooperative. Most players were helpful or neutral, but some would attempt to kill you. This meant that running into other players was tense. Would they heal you? Would they help you take down a machine? Or would they rob you? You had to guess, and guess quickly.

Then there was a big influx of Twitch viewers who were just there for PvP.

I actually think the "mostly cooperative but not always" dynamic is a really interesting vibe, but probably a hard one for the developers to maintain.

在 HN 中查看
michaelbarton 2026/04/16 01:01
还有最近发布的《Marathon》，我认为如果一些 ARC 玩家想要更多的汗流浃背的游戏玩法，他们可能会被吸引。绝对是一种不同的氛围，对双方都有吸引力
There’s also the recent release of Marathon where I think some ARC players might be attracted if they want more of the sweaty gameplay. Definitely a different vibe with appealing aspects to both
在 HN 中查看
moregrist 2026/04/15 20:00
现实生活中有很多这样的例子，从垒球联盟根据严肃性/竞争力/进取性程度进行自我分类，到人们积极避免进入投资银行或高压销售等领域，因为他们以非常进取的名声而闻名。

你不可能总是避开对他人有攻击性的人，但我发现，当我与攻击性的人一起工作时，我的生活压力会更大，所以我积极尝试避免这些情况，并在更具协作性的环境中工作。

There are plenty of real life examples of this, from softball leagues that self-sort based of levels of seriousness / competitiveness / aggressiveness, to people actively avoiding going into areas like investment banking or high-pressure sales because they have a reputation of being very aggressive.

You can’t always avoid people who are aggressive towards others, but I’ve found that my life is a lot more stressful when I work with aggressive people, so I actively try to avoid these situations and work in more collaborative environments.

在 HN 中查看
gavinray 2026/04/16 05:50
这是一个绝妙的主意，逃离塔科夫可能会很好地采用
This is a brilliant idea that Escape from Tarkov might do well to adopt
在 HN 中查看
aggakake 2026/04/15 19:36
人们总是把自己分成派系和泡沫吗？
People sort themselves into cliques and bubbles all the time?
在 HN 中查看
Tade0 2026/04/15 19:40
是的，但是在黑暗的小巷里等你的那个人绝对不是你的派系的成员。
Yes, but the guy waiting for you in a dark alley is definitely not part of your clique.
在 HN 中查看
the_gipsy 2026/04/15 19:41
如果你可以选择 PvP/Coop，那么这将不再是故事。

因此，这是一种尚未披露的事情，使得 TFA ……不再是一个故事。

If you could pick PvP/Coop, then this would be a non-story.

So this is kind of something that has not been disclosed that makes TFA... less of a story.

olalonde 2026/04/15 20:42

The cynic in me is getting "submarine"[0] vibes from this article.

在 HN 中查看
fooker 2026/04/15 18:14
射击是大多数电子游戏的核心机制，这一直让我有点困惑。

这有什么目的吗？

也许这使得参军对于青少年来说并不是太没有吸引力。

It has always puzzled me a little bit that shooting is a core mechanic in a majority of video games.

Does this serve any purpose?

Maybe it makes joining the military not too unappealing for teenagers.

在 HN 中查看
nmeofthestate 2026/04/15 18:35
射击并不是大多数视频游戏的核心机制。
Shooting isn't the core mechanic in the majority of video games.
在 HN 中查看
fooker 2026/04/16 02:24
我很好奇你是否明白大多数意味着什么？
I’m curious if you understand what majority means?
在 HN 中查看
Trasmatta 2026/04/15 19:35
正是这个。任何这么想的人一定几乎没有真正接触过电子游戏。游戏体验的多样性是巨大的，而射击游戏只占其中的一小部分。
Exactly this. Anyone who thinks that must have almost no real exposure to video games. The diversity of gaming experiences is huge, and shooters represent only a small fraction of that.
在 HN 中查看
fooker 2026/04/16 02:25
不同类型的游戏有着长长的尾巴，但有趣的是，总数加起来超过了以射击为主要活动的游戏。
There’s a long tail of diverse genres, but is hilarious to suggest that the total numbers add up to more than games where shooting is the primary activity.
在 HN 中查看
Trasmatta 2026/04/16 03:02
我百分百确定大多数游戏都不涉及任何方式的射击。
I am 100% certain that the majority of games do NOT involve shooting in any way.
在 HN 中查看
wvbdmp 2026/04/15 19:26
事实上，众所周知，顺序是

1. 跑步

2.思考

3. 拍摄

4. 直播

Indeed, as we all know the order is

1. Run

2. Think

3. Shoot

4. Live

在 HN 中查看
user3939382 2026/04/15 19:26
最多产的暴力电子游戏是扫雷。保真度可能较低，但小家伙死了，地雷是游戏中引爆的强大武器。
The most prolific violent video game is Minesweeper. The fidelity may be low but the little guy dies and mines are powerful weapons detonating all over in the game.
在 HN 中查看
hrimfaxi 2026/04/15 21:42
> 最多产的暴力电子游戏是扫雷。

安装基础以什么衡量标准？这似乎不成立。你真的把扫雷游戏等同于第一人称或第三人称射击游戏吗？相比之下，扫雷几乎是人道主义的。

> The most prolific violent video game is Minesweeper.

By what metric, install base? That doesn't seem valid. And are you seriously equating minesweeper with a first or third person shooter? Minesweeper is almost humanitarian in comparison.

在 HN 中查看
yetihehe 2026/04/15 20:50
一个错误就会导致整个关卡爆炸。
One error and the whole level just blows up.
在 HN 中查看
spockz 2026/04/15 18:21
当你被击中时，你会“出局”，并且可以选择带有复活机制的标签游戏是这里小孩子的主要游戏。带有射击功能的视频游戏似乎是虚拟领域的逻辑延伸，并带有一系列“标签”。

除了射击游戏之外，还有许多益智游戏。

Games of tag where you are “out” when hit, optionally with a mechanism for being revived are a staple game for young kids around here. Video games with shooting just seems like a logical extension of that into the virtual domain and with ranged “tag” of that.

Besides shooters there are many puzzle games as well.

在 HN 中查看
pawelduda 2026/04/15 19:17
它通常只是更广泛的难题的一部分 - 你需要精确瞄准，快速反应，正确连锁你的动作，了解周围环境，知道何时进攻/防守，将你的工具/技能应用于特定情况，管理你的资源等等。射击只是所有这些的一个子集。

有了这个逻辑，你也可以将国际象棋简化为杀戮，因为这是核心机制。

It's often just a part of a broader puzzle - you need to aim with precision, react quickly, properly chain your movements, be aware of your surroundings, know when to be offensive/defensive, apply your tools/skills to specific situation, manage your resources, etc. Shooting is just a subset of all that.

With that logic you could also dumb down chess to killing, because that's the core mechanic.

在 HN 中查看
i_c_b 2026/04/15 21:04
游戏机制有很多属性可以促使人们对游戏进行投资。 清晰的规则，清晰的反馈，确定性和离散的因果关系，清晰易懂的获胜条件和游戏状态，在代码中实现相对简单，具有使游戏情境的离散变化和排列易于构建和易于玩家解析的属性，以可以通过实时压力构建的方式设置规则，通常嵌入到大型空间结构中以有机地调整体验......

事实证明，射击（以及更广泛的战斗）很容易满足大多数这些标准。 还有其他核心动作风格也可以做到这一点（例如，2d 平台游戏，或者像俄罗斯方块这样的游戏中的干净谜题机制）。

这些机械因素很重要，因为通常情况下，不喜欢游戏中暴力的人更喜欢游戏关注其他类型的挑战，他们认为这些挑战在道德或意识形态方面对社会更有好处。 但随后他们就践踏了我刚刚在上面列出的问题的机械风格，结果是可以预见的，游戏设计是广大玩家不想玩的。

我曾参与过 AAA 级超级暴力游戏的开发工作，也曾与教育工作者一起研究过他们认为有利于社交的游戏玩法，所以这是我在前排座位上遇到的分歧。

为了形成我希望的富有成效的对比，《Undertale》真正伟大的事情之一是设计师并没有让游戏中的和平变得蹩脚。 尝试弄清楚如何不杀死敌人是（或者对我来说）非常有趣的，因为当你尝试对对手进行心理分析时，你仍然必须进行弹幕地狱躲避，并且躲避（对于喜欢此类机制的玩家）仍然保留了我上面列出的许多属性。

为了进行更现实的比较，我的岳父是一位非常成功的专科大学网球教练，他顺便指出，他个人无法理解如何有人能够像认真对待比赛那样投资花样滑冰这样的奥林匹克运动。 他的论点（特别是他并没有普遍化，只是将其与他作为一名获奖教练的经验联系起来）是，作为一名竞争对手，裁判评分的极端主观性确实让他感到不快。 显然，网球也可能出现糟糕的判罚和其他有争议的裁判问题——所有人类运动都可能发生。 但我认为他的论点与我最初的论点相关；许多游戏玩家确实喜欢干净、清晰的规则，以及清晰的好坏状态，这样他们就可以投资于玩好游戏并享受良好的游戏体验。 而且，正如我所说，此时的射击和战斗通常可以很好地满足这一要求。

There are a lot of properties that game mechanics can have that make people invest in games. Legible rules, clear feedback, deterministic and discrete cause and effect, clearly understandable win conditions and game states, being relatively simple to implement in code, having properties that make discrete variations and permutations of gameplay situations easy to build and easy to parse for players, setting rules up in ways that can be structured with real-time pressure, often embedded in large spatial structures to organically pace an experience...

Shooting (and combat more generally) has proven to be pretty easy to make satisfy most of these criteria. There are other core styles of actions that do as well (say, 2d Platforming, or clean puzzle mechanics like in games like Tetris).

These mechnical factors matter, because it's often the case that people who don't like violence in games would prefer games to focus on other kinds of challenges that they find more socially good in terms of morality or ideology. But then they stomp all over the mechanical styles of issues I was just listing above, and the results is predictably game designs broad masses of players don't want to play.

I've worked on both AAA hyperviolent games, as well as with educators on learning games with what they saw as pro-social game play, so this is a divide I've had front row seats to.

And to make what I hope is a productive contrast, one of the really great things about Undertale is that the designer didn't make being peaceful in the game lame. It is (or was for me) actively fun to try to figure out how to not kill enemies, because you still have to engage in bullet hell dodging while you try to psychoanalyze your opponents, and that dodging (for players who like those kinds of mechanics) still maintained a lot of the properties I just listed above.

To make a more real-world comparison, my father-in-law was an extremely successful junior college tennis coach, and he has noted in passing that he couldn't personally see how anyone could invest in Olympic sports like figure skating, just on the level of taking the competition that seriously. And his argument (he wasn't being universalizing, particularly, just tying it to his experience as an award winning coach) was that the extreme subjectivity of judge ratings was really offputting to him, as a competitor. Obviously tennis can have bad line calls and other controversial judge issues, too - all human sports can. But I think his argument ties in with my original one here; a lot of game players really like clean, legible rules with clear good and bad states so they can invest in getting good at games and take pleasure in their good play. And, as I say, shooting and combat at this point often fulfills that well.

在 HN 中查看
tmtvl 2026/04/15 20:36
我不确定射击是大多数视频游戏的核心机制（也可能取决于你如何定义射击，在射击周围投掷火​​球吗？）。

但除此之外，坎普斯特在他的视频中争论了游戏中的暴力行为（<https://youtu.be/wSBn77_h_6Q>）暴力比非暴力更容易以易于理解的方式进行编程。

I'm not certain that shooting is a core mechanic in a strict majority of video games (may also depend on how you define shooting, is flinging fireballs around shooting?).

But aside from that, Campster argued in his video about violence in games (<https://youtu.be/wSBn77_h_6Q>) that violence is easier to program in an accessible way than nonviolence.

在 HN 中查看
hrimfaxi 2026/04/15 21:43
非暴力比暴力需要更多的理解。
Nonviolence requires more understanding than violence.
在 HN 中查看
baud147258 2026/04/15 20:31
我不认为它背后有任何目的，就像早期一样，射击游戏只是更容易开发，特别是在有限的处理能力和存储方面。例如，我记得原版《毁灭战士》评论中的一段摘录，说如果他们能够与怪物交谈，那就更好了；但在当时，制作一款会说话的游戏几乎是不可能的，尤其是要达到与原版《毁灭战士》相同的打磨水平。

然后这是一个反馈循环：视频游戏获得了暴力的名声（也许这是不值得的，就像《神秘岛》的销量超过了原版《毁灭战士》、《IIRC》，但暴力游戏在主流媒体上占据了更大的头条新闻）=>只有对此感兴趣的人才会购买=>暴力游戏是最畅销的=>游戏...

I don't think there's any purpose behind it, most like early on, game with shooting were just simpler to develop, especially with regards to limited processing power and storage. For example I remember an extract from a review on the original Doom, saying that it would be much better if they were able to talk to the monsters; but at the time, a talking game would have been nearly impossible to make, especially to the same level of polish as the original Doom.

And then it's a feedback loop: video games get the reputation of being violent (perhaps undeservedly so, like Myst was outselling the original Doom, IIRC, but violent games made for bigger headline in mainstream media) => only people interested in that buy them => violent games are the best-selling => games...

在 HN 中查看
hyperman1 2026/04/16 03:11
我喜欢《哥特 1》的一件事是你可以与人战斗而不杀死他们（杀死是你获胜后的额外动作）。NPC 对获胜和杀死的反应不同，促使你让别人活下去。在残酷的流放地，这比胜利=杀戮更有意义。
One thing I liked about Gothic 1 was how you could fight people and not kill them ( Kill was an extra action after you won).The NPCs reacted differently to winning vs killing, pushing you to let others live. In a brutal penal colony, this made a lot more sense than win=kill.
在 HN 中查看
chamomeal 2026/04/15 20:25
我认为这更多是因为点击式游戏更适合射击游戏。看看你想拍摄的东西，点击它。一个简单的前提，你可以将东西分层来制作一个好的游戏。
I think it’s more because point-and-click is suited to shooters. Look at thing you want to shoot, click on it. A simple premise that you can layer stuff onto to make a good game.
在 HN 中查看
astoor 2026/04/15 19:15
> “射击是大多数电子游戏的核心机制，这一直让我有点困惑。这有什么用处吗？

我个人的理论是，在高度军事化的社会中，暴力视频游戏（以及电影和其他媒体）受到鼓励，以使人们对暴力不再敏感——如果你将其正常化，让一切看起来像游戏或其他形式的娱乐，那么当你在其他国家杀害真人时，你会得到更少的内部反对。

> "It has always puzzled me a little bit that shooting is a core mechanic in a majority of video games. Does this serve any purpose?

My personal theory is that violent video games (and films and other media) are encouraged in highly militarised societies to desensitise their populations to violence - if you normalise it so it all seems like a game or other form of entertainment, you get a lot less internal opposition when you go about killing real people in other countries.

在 HN 中查看
pawelduda 2026/04/15 19:32
我只是没有看到暴力电影或游戏背后的通常团队有一个“我们怎样才能让人们更想参加战争”的思考过程。我的理论有点相反——人们喜欢这样的媒体，因为它是暴力，不会伤害现实世界中的任何人，是一种幻想。
I just don't see a usual team behind a violent movie or game having a though process of "how can we make people want to go to war more". My theory is sort of the opposite - people enjoy such media because it's violence without hurting anyone in the real world, a fantasy.
在 HN 中查看
trinsic2 2026/04/15 19:39
唔。恕我直言，这个做法有点过分了。我认为这就像任何运动一样。竞赛。战斗策略是一种相当多样化的竞争方式。
Hmm. This take is a bit much IMHO. I think it just like any sport. Competition. Combat tactics is a pretty diverse way to play competitively.
在 HN 中查看
cindyllm 2026/04/16 04:37 deleted

在 HN 中查看
kreco 2026/04/15 19:40
我不介意拍摄。

但我确实介意射杀人类。我希望我们在这方面能更有创意。

I don't mind shooting.

But I do mind shooting human being. I wish we would be more creative on that front.

在 HN 中查看
Insanity 2026/04/15 20:01
有些游戏探讨了射击狗的问题（CoD 和 BF 都曾在某个阶段 iirc 做过这个）。对此的反应比射杀人类要负面得多。

编辑：但我知道你的意思是射击外星人或类似的东西。

Some games explored shooting dogs (CoD and BF both had this at some stage iirc). The reception to that was much more negative than shooting humans.

Edit: but I know you mean shooting aliens or something similar.

在 HN 中查看
darkteflon 2026/04/15 18:44
我的意思是，真正优秀的人可以是美丽的、可怕的、芭蕾舞般的统治力、技巧和战术智慧的展示。在所有游戏中，没有什么比被人类追捕更重要的了。 这真是令人兴奋。
I mean, the really good ones can be beautiful, terrifying, balletic displays of dominance, skill and tactical intelligence. There’s nothing in all of gaming quite like being hunted by a human being. It’s a real thrill.
在 HN 中查看
rolandog 2026/04/15 20:02
是的，尽管我认为，如果几年前由于“觉醒恐慌”而进行的同样的审查适用于现代有关支持你命名的宣传（军事、其他等）的内容，我们作为一个社会会变得更好。

如今，你会看到新闻报道时对事实的巧妙遗漏（例如，为什么在谈论某些国家的贫困和不稳定时没有提到非法贸易禁运？为什么当他们自信地展示的东西被证明是假的或 GenAI 时没有反应？），或者电子游戏中描绘事物的方式（为什么军事射击游戏中的敌人几乎总是中东人？为什么你不必与种族主义者、法西斯主义者和企业进行战斗）民兵？），或者电影（为什么我们看到的大部分内容都是由一个人独自承担对抗单个恶棍的责任？）。

抱歉，我的咆哮；游戏确实很美……有些事情我已经开始注意，你必须吞下或抛开一些宣传，这样你才能被允许和你的朋友一起玩……这让我有点不安。

Yeah, though I would argue that we as a society would be way better off if the same scrutiny that was applied a few years ago due to the "woke panic" were applied to modern day content about pro- you-name-it propaganda (military, othering, etc.).

Nowadays, you see that in the masterful omission of facts when news are reported (e.g. why aren't illegal trade embargoes mentioned when talking about poverty and instability in certain countries? Why are there no reactions when the thing they were confidently showing turned out to be false or GenAI?), or the way things are portrayed in videogames (why are enemies in military shooters almost always middle eastern? why don't you have to fight off racists, fascists, and corporate militia?), or the movies (why do we get shown mostly content where a single individual carries the sole responsibility of taking on the single villain?).

Sorry for the rant; games are indeed beautiful... There's some things I've been starting to pay attention to where you have to swallow or brush aside some propaganda so that you're allowed to play with your friends... And that makes me a bit upset.

在 HN 中查看
scotty79 2026/04/15 18:38
它是指点和点击。这只是游戏可以让玩家做的最简单的事情之一。大致应该发出什么声音以及呈现什么视觉效果是很直观的。

奇怪的是，大多数舞蹈都会把一只脚放在另一只脚前面。

It's pointing and clicking. It's just one of the simplest things a game can make a player do. It's intuitive what sound roughly it should make and what visual effect to show up.

It's as if it was weird that most dancing has a lot of putting one foot in front of the other.

在 HN 中查看
philipwhiuk 2026/04/15 19:22
其实并没有那么复杂。

从历史上看，人类的竞争能力比人工智能更好。编写与人类势均力敌的人工智能，以提出一个艰难但并非无法获胜的挑战，比仅仅与另一个人对抗要困难得多。

> 也许这使得参军对于青少年来说并不是太没有吸引力。

有人应该告诉美国陆军： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/America%27s_Army

（令人惊讶的是，对于一个政府项目来说，它的可玩性相当高）

It's not really that complex.

Humans have historically been better competition than AI. Writing AI that is evenly matched with a human so as presenting a challenge that is tough but not unwinnable is much harder than just playing against another person.

> Maybe it makes joining the military not too unappealing for teenagers.

Someone should have told the US Army: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/America%27s_Army

(Surprisingly for a government project it was pretty playable)

在 HN 中查看
Tade0 2026/04/15 19:53
> 编写与人类势均力敌的人工智能，以提出一个艰难但并非不可获胜的挑战，这比仅仅与另一个人对抗要困难得多。

人类也是独一无二的……人类。

我扮演的是一名撤离射手，甚至是一名排名更高的玩家，通常在公开战斗中击倒我的三人小队不会有任何问题，但如果我们躲在周围骚扰他们，最终会被累垮。此外，他们可能会更早失去耐心并因此开始犯错误。

很难对这样的事情进行建模，因为人们是不同的并且以复杂的方式做出反应。

> Writing AI that is evenly matched with a human so as presenting a challenge that is tough but not unwinnable is much harder than just playing against another person.

Also humans are uniquely... human.

I play one of those extraction shooters and even a much higher ranked player, who would normally have no issue downing my team of three in an open fight, will eventually get worn down if we hide around and harass them. Also they might just lose patience earlier and start making mistakes due to that.

Hard to model something like this because people are different and react in complex ways.

在 HN 中查看
sublinear 2026/04/15 19:13
任何映射到熟练的手眼协调游戏的简单输入方案通常都适用，甚至是古老的街机游戏。

当游戏大多是 2D 时，许多动作游戏都被整合到平台游戏中。射击游戏似乎与 3D 游戏类型相同。

Any simple input scheme mapped to a game of skilled hand-eye coordination usually holds up, even ancient arcade games.

Back when games were mostly 2D, a lot of action games got consolidated into platformers. Shooters just seem to be the equivalent genre in 3D.

在 HN 中查看
[deleted] 2026/04/15 20:49 deleted

[已删除]

在 HN 中查看
klondike_klive 2026/04/15 18:50
不久前，我在 Wii 上玩了《生化危机》。我发现自己真的厌倦了尿液和粪便的颜色水平和无情的痛苦（也许我应该知道比租《生化危机》更好！）

我的想法是采用经典的 3D 电子游戏关卡，并用鲜花和长凳进行美化。执行功能战胜了我，但我仍然时不时地思考它。

A while ago when I got Resident Evil on the Wii. I found myself really fed up and depressed with the pee and poo coloured levels and relentless misery (maybe I should have known better than to rent Resident Evil!)

I had the idea of taking classic 3d videogame levels and landscaping them with flowers and benches. Executive function got the better of me, I but I still muse about it from time to time.

在 HN 中查看
lamasery 2026/04/15 21:09
我真的非常希望我的儿子能够在《孤岛惊魂 5》中加入非暴力模式。如果存在消除敌人和武器的模式，我可以保证这将是我儿子（当时他大概六岁？）连续几年最喜欢的游戏。乘坐飞机或驾车游览蒙大拿州的乡村风光、钓鱼、观赏野生动物？他在这种模式下花费的时间比我平常通关游戏的时间多 20 倍。两人本地多人游戏的超级奖励积分，这样我们就可以一起玩。

（令人沮丧的是 关闭 如果你在通关后继续玩，但仍然有枪，并且仍然有一些敌对分子在周围徘徊，IIRC）

I really, really wanted a non-violent mode in Far Cry 5 for my son. If a mode that removed the hostiles and weapons had existed I can guarantee that would have been my son’s (he was probably about 6 at the time?) favorite game for a couple years straight. Flying and driving around rural Montana landscapes, fishing, watching wildlife? He’d have spent 20x more time in that mode than I did beating the game ordinarily. Super bonus points for two-player local multiplayer so we could play together.

(It came frustratingly close to this if you kept playing after beating the game, but there were still guns and still some hostiles wandering around, IIRC)

在 HN 中查看
thebruce87m 2026/04/15 19:47
我一直在寻找非暴力、低压力的游戏。我确实喜欢探索。像《短途远足》这样的游戏很少见。

我以为《死亡搁浅》的压力应该较小，但我已经很接近开始了，到目前为止我还得担心物品退化、库存管理和敌人。我只是在寻找更多平静的步行模拟器游戏。我希望更多的游戏有非战斗选项，或者可能是“耶稣掌舵”模式，以应对压力较大的情况，将其变成更多的交互式过场动画。

I’m always on the lookout for non-violent low-stress games. I do like exploration. Games like A Short Hike are few and far between.

I thought Death Stranding was supposed to be less-stressful but I’m quite near the start and so far I’ve got to worry about items degrading, inventory management and enemies. I was just looking for more of a peaceful walking simulator game. I wish more games had a non-combat option, or maybe a “Jesus take the wheel” mode for the stressful bits that turns it into more of an interactive cutscene.

在 HN 中查看
happosai 2026/04/15 22:06
有很多非暴力低压力游戏。找到好的人只是一个挑战。一些我喜欢的

- 精神农夫 - 拉娜星球 - 远：单独销售 - 潜水员戴夫 - 拉力赛的艺术

There are lots of non-violent low stress games. It's just a challenge to find the good ones. Some ones I have enjoyed

- spirit farmer - planet of Lana - far: lone sales - Dave the diver - art of rally

yetihehe 2026/04/15 20:43
尝试“天空：光之子”。或者只是观看我见过的最好的游戏预告片之一。 www.youtube.com/watch?v=A3DNTfhUy0k
Try "Sky: Children of the light". Or just watch one of the best game trailers I've seen. www.youtube.com/watch?v=A3DNTfhUy0k

nemomarx 2026/04/15 20:22
您可能想要更接近 Powerwash sim 的东西？
You might like something closer to powerwash sim?
在 HN 中查看
Insanity 2026/04/15 20:00
早在 2000 年代初，许多带有专用服务器浏览器的游戏在晚上就变成了聊天室。我已经看到，即使是在本文所说的只是“射击”的游戏中，比如CS。但它发生在ET、CoD、JK等身上。
Many games with a dedicated servers browser back in the early 2000s just turned into chatrooms at night. I’ve seen that even in games that this article calls out as being just about “shooting” like cs. But it happened on ET, CoD, JK, etc.
在 HN 中查看
melonpan7 2026/04/16 04:16
我一直对《Arc Raiders》很着迷，因为现在我更多地是一名单人玩家。下次发售时可能会尝试一下，特别是因为我听说它在 Linux 上运行完美。
I’ve always been fascinated about Arc Raiders since I’m more of a solo player these days. Might try it out next time it’s on sale, especially since I heard it runs flawless on Linux.
在 HN 中查看
moribvndvs 2026/04/15 20:55
最近这种情况有改变吗？我在发布时就开始玩游戏，虽然最初很有趣，而且我主要获得了积极的体验，但几周后我退出了，因为它变成了悲伤。我不介意这里有点出汗，但我没有时间让边缘领主在他们的 3 位抽搐观众面前成为蝇王。
Has this changed recently? I started playing at launch, and while initially it was a lot of fun and I had predominantly positive experiences, after a couple of weeks I quit because it devolved into little more than griefing. I don’t mind it getting a little sweaty here and there but I don’t have time for nonstop edgelords going lord of the flies in front of their 3 twitch viewers.
在 HN 中查看
anthonj 2026/04/15 19:10
我记得《绝地武士》系列中的许多在线服务器上，人们会花很多时间交谈（嗯……在聊天中写作）、闲逛、进行友好的决斗或探索奇怪的自定义 mmap。

考虑到游戏类型，这有点奇怪。

I remember a lot of online servers from the Jedi Knight series where people would spend a lot of time talking (well.. writing in chat), messing around, making friendly duels or exploring weird custom mmaps.

It was kinda weird considering the game type.

在 HN 中查看
deflator 2026/04/15 23:13
让我想起一年前《半条命》的合作模式。它完全消除了 PvP，让数十名玩家对抗谜题和一波又一波的敌人。
Reminds me of the coop mod for Half Life 1 years back. It completely removed PvP and set dozens of players against puzzles and waves of enemies.

jareklupinski 2026/04/16 02:08
《It Takes a War》进入多人动作游戏的社交部分： https://store.steampowered.com/app/3919530/It_Takes_a_War/

（商店页面和游戏视频故意显得迟钝，这是一种“毫无预知地投入以获得最佳结果”的游戏之一）

"It Takes a War" gets into the social part of multiplayer action games: https://store.steampowered.com/app/3919530/It_Takes_a_War/

(the store page and gameplay videos are purposely obtuse, its one of those 'dive in with no precognition for best results' kind of games)

homeonthemtn 2026/04/15 19:51
希望我们能绕一圈回到游戏大厅。游戏大厅和固定服务器是本文讨论的微型社区。它在早期运行得非常好。我知道我当然想念它
Hopefully we come full circle back to game lobbies. Game lobbies and fixed servers were the micro communities this article is dancing around. It worked remarkably well in the early days. I know I certainly miss it
在 HN 中查看
dude250711 2026/04/15 18:38
Mordhau 有点像这样。我们现在还需要《军团要塞 3》。
Mordhau is a bit like this. We also need Team Fortress 3 now.
在 HN 中查看
intended 2026/04/15 20:47
电子游戏因其所提供的各种迷你实验和对人性的洞察而令人难以置信。

玩家驱动的经济就是一个很好的例子。每个人都以相同的水平和相同的差点加入游戏。然而，你最终会得到与财富集中相同的幂律动力学。

游戏也是对网络毒性进行真正研究的行业之一，也是最积极寻找平衡方法的行业之一。

这让《弧线攻略》中的合作频率有些反常。

Video games are incredible for the kinds of mini experiments and insights on human nature they throw up.

Player driven economies are a case in point. Everyone joins the game at the same level, and same handicap. Yet you end up with the same power law dynamics of wealth concentration.

Gaming is also one industry that does real research on online toxicity, and one of the more motivated sectors to finding a way to balance it out.

This is something that makes the frequency of cooperation in Arc Raiders somewhat anomalous.

在 HN 中查看
intended 2026/04/15 20:43
在最近推出的所有视频游戏中，《Arc raiders》非常有趣。

Cohh Carnage 的《Arc Raider》在直播中看到了许多真正精彩的互动，尤其是在橡皮鸭奔跑中。

有趣的是，单人 Arc Raiders 与团队 Arc Raiders 有着截然不同的氛围。在我观看的几个基于小组的直播中，玩家默认互相射击。单人赛的竞争力较差。

Of all the recent video games, Arc raiders has been just good fun to watch.

Cohh Carnage’s Arc Raider streams and saw many genuinely nice interactions, especially on rubber duck runs.

Interestingly, Solo Arc Raiders has a very different vibe than team Arc Raiders. In the few group based streams I watched, players shot each other by default. In solo it was less competitive.

在 HN 中查看
incomingpain 2026/04/15 20:01
友好的 Hoovies 提供和平三明治和交谈。

下一关，你要医治并治愈那些家伙。通过不战斗自动赢得每一轮。

Friendly Hoovies offer peace Sandviches and talk.

Next level, you medic and heal the hoovies. Winning every round automatically by not fighting.

在 HN 中查看
strangegecko 2026/04/15 20:16
在我看来，这对团队的其他成员非常不友好，因为他们不同意部分团队拒绝参加比赛。

在不破坏时间平衡的情况下，还有很多其他方式可以聊天和结交朋友。

IMO this is aggressively unfriendly to the rest of the team that didn't consent to part of the team refusing to play.

There are so many other ways to chat and make friends without breaking tem balance.

在 HN 中查看
incomingpain 2026/04/16 01:17
这是TF2。别那么严肃的事情。

如果你这么在乎平衡的话就去玩CS吧。

It's TF2. Dont be so serious business.

Go play CS if you care so much about balance.

在 HN 中查看
